remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Relapse in 19-45% within 1 year of antidepressant discontinuation after remission; relapse risk ratio (off-medication vs on) falls from 3.69x at 2 months to 1.34x at 5 years
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: 55-80% remain relapse-free at 1 year after well-managed discontinuation; psychotherapy (CBT) achieves comparable remission with better durability; psilocybin-assisted therapy showing 29-71% response at 3 months in treatment-resistant cases
remission.gap_size: Moderate; narrows substantially over time for maintenance medication question
remission.barrier_detail: Guidelines push long maintenance durations partly because short-term relapse risk is high, but long-term maintenance evidence is weaker than commonly assumed; psychotherapy significantly underutilized relative to medication
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — FDA approved esketamine (Spravato) nasal spray for TRD; 2023 FDA approved psilocybin-assisted therapy pathway (Australian TGA 2023, US FDA Breakthrough designation); first-line SSRIs unchanged since early 1990s
remission.outdated_treatments: Tricyclic antidepressants (TCAs) in routine first-line use; benzodiazepine co-prescribing as long-term adjunct (dependence risk, no antidepressant effect); antipsychotic augmentation before psychotherapy is attempted
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Exercise (meta-analysis: comparable to SSRI for mild-moderate depression); phototherapy (for non-seasonal depression, underutilized); anti-inflammatory interventions (NSAIDs, omega-3) for inflammatory subtype; vagus nerve stimulation (FDA-cleared 2005, coverage finally added 2023)
remission.frontier_signal: Psilocybin-assisted therapy (COMP360, FDA Breakthrough); MDMA-assisted therapy (FDA review ongoing); rapid-acting glutamatergic agents (ketamine analogs); transcranial magnetic stimulation (expanded protocols)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
